supporting_evidence: Expense date difference is 8 days (2025-10-01 to 2025-10-09), exceeding the 7-day threshold in Article 11
No other E0010 travel_lodging records exist within ±7 days of either record (only R004236 and R004237 in full Sep 24 – Oct 16 window)
Different invoices: FP2025X0004230 and FP2025X0004231 — no duplicate invoice
No special_approval on either record, and none needed since rule not triggered
opposing_evidence: Same employee (E0010), same expense type (travel_lodging), same vendor (安程酒店管理有限公司), same amount (¥5,200), same nights (7) — pattern is suggestive
Combined ¥10,400 reaches AR-03 higher approval threshold
Sequential stays (Oct 1-8 and Oct 9-16) suggest a continuous project presence
8-day interval is very close to the 7-day boundary

exceptions_or_boundaries.detail: 8 days vs 7-day threshold. Even with most conservative inclusive counting (Oct 1 = day 1, Oct 9 = day 9), the span is 9 days, exceeding 7. The interval of 8 days is a genuine exceedance.

rationale: The Article 11 split reimbursement rule requires ALL three conditions: (1) same employee + same expense type, (2) within 7 days, (3) combined amount reaches higher approval line. Condition (2) is not met: the 8-day gap between expense dates (2025-10-01 and 2025-10-09) exceeds the 7-day threshold. While the pattern is suspicious (same vendor, same amount, sequential stays suggesting continuous project presence), the rule's text is clear and the boundary is genuinely exceeded. No anomaly.
